mnemonics: SLAY Optimization: Solve, Locate, Analyze, Yield. A mnemonic for the process of solving (formulate problem), locating (find feasible region), analyzing (test vertices), and yielding (interpret the optimal solution).
keyPoints: Linear Programming problems involve maximizing or minimizing a linear objective function subject to linear inequality constraints.
The Fundamental Theorem of Linear Programming states that if an optimal solution exists, it must occur at a vertex (corner point) of the feasible region.
The Simplex Method is an algorithmic procedure that moves from one vertex of the feasible set to an adjacent vertex with a better objective value until the optimum is found.
commonMistakes: Misinterpreting slack variables as having a real-world meaning beyond converting inequalities to equations for the simplex tableau.
Forgetting to check for alternative optimal solutions when the objective function is parallel to a binding constraint.
Failing to identify an unbounded problem when the feasible region is open and the objective function can improve indefinitely.
